questions.question:
Based on the clues given below, guess Who am I?

  1. Three-fourths of the earth surface is covered by this
  2. An important renewable natural resource
  3. Though primarily used for drinking and washing, also used for productive purposes like irrigation, generation of electricity, etc
questions.explanation: Water is a vital renewable natural resource. Three-fourths of the earth’s surface is covered with water. Humans use huge amounts of water not only for drinking and washing but also in the process of production. Water for agriculture, industries, generating electricity.

questions.question:
Cite one good reason as to why Earth is called Water Planet.

  1. Due to the fact that water is a vital renewable resource.
  2. Due to the fact that water is responsible for moderate temperature conditions.
  3. Due to the fact that three fourth's of the earth's surface is covered with water.
  4. Due to the fact that water can neither be added nor subtracted from the earth.
questions.explanation: Water is a vital renewable natural resource. Three-fourths of the earth’s surface is covered with water. It is therefore appropriately called the ‘water planet’.

questions.question: A few important facts about Oceans are given below. Identify the one that is not true:

options.html: Oceans support a rich variety of plant and animal life
Oceans cover two-thirds of the earth’s surface
Life began in the ocean almost 3.5 billion years ago

questions.explanation: The ocean water is saline and not fit for human consumption. Fresh water accounts for only about 2.7 per cent. Only 1 per cent of fresh water is available and fit for human use.

questions.question:
Pick out the one statement that is not true with respect to the details given about Fresh Water.

  1. Freshwater on the earth's surface is only about 1.7 per cent of the total water.
  2. About 70% of the freshwater occurs as ice-sheets and glaciers.
  3. Only about one per cent of fresh water is available and fit for human use.
  4. Due to the increasing demand of the increasing population, there is always a shortage of fresh water.

options.html: About 70% of the freshwater occurs as ice-sheets and glaciers.
Due to the increasing demand of the increasing population, there is always a shortage of freshwater.
Freshwater on the earth's surface is only about 1.7 percent of the total water.
Only about one percent of freshwater is available and fit for human use.

questions.explanation: Freshwater accounts for only about 2.7 percent.

questions.question: Water can neither be added nor subtracted from the earth. It volume remains constant abundance may seem to vary because it is in constant motion, cycling through the oceans, the air, land, and back again, through the processes of evaporation, precipitation, and run-off. What is this entire process called?
questions.explanation: Freshwater is the most precious substance on earth. Water can neither be added nor subtracted from the earth. Its total volume remains constant. Its abundance only seems to vary because it is in constant motion, cycling through the oceans, the air, the land, and back again, through the processes of evaporation, precipitation, and run-off. This as you already know is referred to as the ‘water cycle’.

questions.question: Some important rivers of India are given below. Select the one that is not in India.
questions.explanation: The Volga is the longest river in Europe.

questions.question: A few important facts with respect to the Dams built across the rivers are given below. Pick out the one that is not applicable.

options.html: Dams also help in generating hydroelectricity
Water stored in the dams cannot be used for drinking

questions.explanation: Dams are built not just for irrigation but for electricity generation, drinking, water supply for domestic and industrial uses, flood control, recreation, inland navigation, and fish breeding.

questions.question: This method effectively irrigates the area by checking water losses through seepage and evaporation in dry areas. With the help of the image given, name this irrigation method.
questions.explanation: Water Sprinklers effectively irrigate the  area by checking water losses through seepage and evaporation. In dry regions with high rates of evaporation, drip or trickle irrigation is very useful.

questions.question: Constructing tanks and percolation pits, an important method to conserve water by saving surface runoff. Name this method with the help of the image given
questions.explanation:
Rain water harvesting is the process of
collecting rain water from roof tops and directing it to an appropriate location and storing if for future use.

questions.question: Name the life-bearing layer of the earth.
questions.explanation: Natural vegetation and wildlife exist only in the narrow zone of contact between the lithosphere, hydrosphere, and atmosphere that we call the biosphere. In the biosphere living beings are interrelated and interdependent on each other for survival.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a major vegetation type of the world?
questions.explanation: The major vegetation types of the world are grouped as forests, grasslands, scrubs, and tundra.

questions.question: ________ forests do not shed their leaves simultaneously in any season of the year.
questions.explanation: Forests are broadly classified as evergreen and deciduous depending on when they shed their leaves. Evergreen forests do not shed their leaves simultaneously in any season of the year.

questions.question: ________ forests shed their leaves in a particular season to conserve loss of moisture through transpiration.
questions.explanation: Forests are broadly classified as evergreen and deciduous depending on when they shed their leaves. Deciduous forests shed their leaves in a particular season to conserve loss of moisture through transpiration.

questions.question:
Full form of CITES:

  1. The Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora
  2. The Convention on International Threatened Species of Wild Fauna and Flora
  3. The Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species
  4. The Community on International Trade in Endangered Species
questions.explanation: CITES (the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora) is an international agreement (between governments) that aims to ensure that international trade in specimens of wild animals and plants does not threaten their survival. Roughly 5,000 species of animals and 28,000 species of plants are protected. Bears, dolphins, cacti, corals, orchids and aloes are some examples.

questions.question:
Pick out the wrong statement from the following:

  1. CITES is an international agreement between governments.
  2. CITES support the species of animals and birds whose trade is prohibited.
  3. Roughly about 5,000 animals and 25,000 plant species are protected under CITES.
  4. CITES seeks to ensure that international trade in wild animal and plant species does not threaten their survival.
questions.explanation: CITES (the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora) is an international agreement between governments. It aims to ensure that international trade in specimens of wild animals and plants does not threaten their survival. Roughly 5,000 species of animals and 28,000 species of plants are protected. Bears, dolphins, cacti, corals, orchids, and aloes are some examples.

questions.question: What would you call a naturally occurring substance that has a definite chemical composition?
questions.explanation: Minerals are formed in different types of geological environments, under varying conditions. A naturally occurring substance that has a definite chemical composition is a mineral.

questions.question:
Listed below are a few important facts about Minerals. Which one among the following is not true.

  1. Minerals are evenly distributed over space.
  2. Minerals are concentrated in a particular area or rock formations.
  3. Some minerals are found in areas that are not easily accessible.
  4. Minerals are composed of one or two elements.
questions.explanation: Minerals are not evenly distributed over space.

questions.question: Minerals can be identified on the basis of their physical properties. Which one among the following is not a physical property with respect to the minerals?
questions.explanation: Minerals can be identified on the basis of their physical properties such as colour, density, hardness.

questions.question: Which one of the following best defines a rock which contains enough mineral to make it economically viable for mining?
questions.explanation: An ore is a rock that contains a large enough concentration of a particular mineral - often a metal to make it profitable for mining and processing.

questions.question: In the ________ grade ores, the mineral content is high and impurities are less. In the ________ grade ores, mineral content is low and impurities are more.
questions.explanation: High-grade ore contains a large concentration of the desired mineral. It has fewer impurities low-grade ore has a smaller concentration. It has more impurities.

questions.question: Complete the following on the basis of Composition, Minerals can be classified as :
questions.explanation: There are over three thousand different minerals. On the basis of composition, minerals are classified mainly as metallic and non-metallic minerals.

questions.question: Analyse the following and complete the blank
questions.explanation: There are over three thousand different minerals. On the basis of composition there are metallic minerals and further Metallic minerals may be ferrous or non-ferrous.

questions.question: Which one of the following contains metal in raw form?
questions.explanation: Metals are hard substances that conduct heat and electricity and have a characteristic lustre or shine. The metallic minerals contain metal in raw form.

questions.question:
Which one of the following best describes a Metal?

  1. Substances without chemical composition.
  2. Substances without any physical properties.
  3. Hard substances that conduct heat and electricity and have a characteristic lustre or shine.
  4. A substance without any definite shape.
questions.explanation:
Properties of Metals:

  1. They are hard substances.
  2. Good conductor of heat and electricity.
  3. They have a characteristic lustre or shine.

questions.question: Some examples of minerals are given below, pick out the one that is not a metallic mineral.
questions.explanation: The metallic minerals contain metal in raw form. Iron ore, bauxite, manganese ore are some examples. Limestone is a non-metallic mineral.

questions.question: Some examples of minerals are given below, pick out the one that is not a non-metallic mineral.
questions.explanation: The non-metallic minerals do not contain metals. Limestone, mica, and gypsum are examples of such minerals. Gold is a metallic mineral.

questions.question: Give two examples of non-metallic mineral fuels from the options given below:
questions.explanation: Mineral fuels like coal and petroleum are non-metallic minerals.

questions.question: Which of these methods are widely used for extracting minerals:
questions.explanation: Minerals can be extracted by mining, drilling, or quarrying.

questions.question: From the following options given, pick out the correct reason as to why impurities should be removed from mineral ores?

options.html: To obtain large quantities of mineral ores
To convert mineral ores into useful materials
To retain the minerals in its pure state

questions.explanation: By removing impurities, the impure minerals ore can be converted into a vital material.

questions.question: Apart from the given two methods, name one more method that is used for Extraction of Minerals
questions.explanation: Minerals can be extracted by mining, drilling or quarrying .

questions.question: Name the process of taking out minerals from rocks buried under the surface of the earth.
questions.explanation: Minerals can be extracted by the process of mining. The process of taking out minerals from rocks buried under the earth’s surface is called mining.

questions.question: Name this process of extracting minerals. Here minerals that lie at shallow depths are taken out by removing the surface layer from an open pit.
questions.explanation: The process of taking out minerals from rocks buried under the earth’s surface is called mining. Minerals that lie at shallow depths are taken out by removing the surface layer; this is known as open-cast mining.

questions.question: From the image attached identify the method used to extract minerals. Deep bores have to be made to reach mineral deposits that lie at great depths.
questions.explanation: The process of taking out minerals from rocks buried under the earth’s surface is called mining. Deep bores, called shafts, have to be made to reach mineral deposits that lie at great depths. This is called shaft mining.

questions.question: Name the method used to extract Petroleum and natural gas wherein deep wells are bored to take them out.
questions.explanation: Petroleum and natural gas occur far below the earth’s surface. Deep wells are bored to take them out, this is called drilling.

questions.question: Name the country with the largest reserve of iron ore.
questions.explanation: Australia the world's largest iron ore producers and hold a large portion of the world's iron ore reserves.

questions.question: A few examples of minerals found in different areas are given below. Pick out the one that is not an example of mineral found in igneous and metamorphic rocks.

options.html: Iron, Nickel, Chromites in South Africa
Copper and Nickel deposits in Ontario

questions.explanation: Sedimentary rock formations of plains and young fold mountains contain non-metallic minerals like limestone. Limestone deposits of the Caucasus region of France, manganese deposits of Georgia and Ukraine and phosphate beds of Algeria are some examples.

questions.question: Listed below are a few examples of minerals found in different areas. Pick out the one that is not an example of mineral found in sedimentary strata.

options.html: Copper and Nickel deposits in Ontario
Limestone deposits in Caucasus Mountains of France

questions.explanation: Copper and nickel deposits in Ontario is an example of minerals found in igneous and metamorphic rocks. Sedimentary rock formations of plains and young fold mountains contain non-metallic minerals like limestone. Limestone deposits of the Caucasus region of France, manganese deposits of Georgia, and Ukraine and phosphate beds of Algeria are some examples.

questions.question: Name the country where there are no known mineral deposits in the world.
questions.explanation: Minerals deposits are located in France, Canada, Algeria. There are no known deposits of minerals in Switzerland.

questions.question: Name the two countries known to have large iron ore deposits in Asia.
questions.explanation: China and India have large iron ore deposits. The continent produces more than half of the world’s tin.

questions.question: Which among the following is not a leading producer of tin in the world?
questions.explanation: China, Malaysia, and Indonesia are among the world’s leading tin producers. Sri Lanka is not on this list.

questions.question: Which country leads in the production of lead, antimony, and tungsten?
questions.explanation: China leads in production of lead, antimony and tungsten.

questions.question: Which of the following countries do not have huge reserves of petroleum and natural gas?
questions.explanation: Huge reserves of Petroleum and natural gas are found in Saudi Arabia, Iran, and Iraq. China doesn't have such reserves.

questions.question: Which continent on the world has rich deposits of manganese, bauxite, nickel, zinc, and copper?
questions.explanation: Asia also has deposits of manganese, bauxite, nickel, zinc, and copper.

questions.question: From the given countries, which one does not have large deposits of iron ore in the world?
questions.explanation: Large deposits of iron ore in the world are found in Ukraine, Russia, and Sweden. India doesn't have abundant iron ore.

questions.question: Name the country in Europe where the Ruhr Valley is situated. Rich in coal deposits, this region is today one of the most populated industrial regions.
questions.explanation: The Ruhr is a large coal field located in the west of Germany in North Rhine-Westphalia.

questions.question: Pick out the region of North America where huge mineral deposits are found.
questions.explanation: The Appalachians are the mountains in eastern North America where huge mineral deposits are found.

questions.question: Name the mineral largely found in the Canadian Shield region of North America.
questions.explanation: The Canadian Shield is one of the world's richest areas in terms of mineral ores Nickel.

questions.question: Name the mineral deposits found in the Appalachian region of North America.
questions.explanation: The Appalachian region of North America is rich in coal deposits.

questions.question: Name the country with the largest producer of high-grade iron ore in the world.
questions.explanation: Brazil has rich high-grade iron ore and is the largest producer of high-grade iron ore in the world.

questions.question: Pick out from the list the two important producers of copper in the world.
questions.explanation: In South America, Chile and Peru are leading producers of copper.

questions.question: Choose the largest producers of tin in the world:
questions.explanation: In South America, Brazil and Bolivia are among the world’s largest producers of tin.

questions.question: Name the largest producer of precious minerals like diamond, gold silver, and platinum.
questions.explanation: Africa has abundant deposits of precious metals and it is the largest producer of precious minerals like diamond, gold silver, and platinum.

questions.question: In the last 100 years, 50% of the world's gold has come from this region. Pick out the name of the region from the list given below.
questions.explanation: The Witwatersrand Gold Rush was a gold rush in 1886 that led to the establishment of Johannesburg, South Africa. In the last 100 years, 50% of the world's gold has come from this region.

questions.question: Name the continent with the largest producer of bauxite in the world.
questions.explanation: Australia leads to the production of bauxite in the world.

questions.question: Large deposits of gold are found in Kalgoorlie and Coolgardie. Which part of the Australian continent are they situated?
questions.explanation: Kalgoorlie and Coolgardie areas of western Australia have the largest deposits of gold.

questions.question: Name the region in Antarctica where significant deposits of coal are found?
questions.explanation: The significant size of deposits of coal in the Transantarctic Mountains and iron near the Prince Charles Mountains of East Antarctica is forecasted.

questions.question: Pick out the region in India where iron-ore deposits are not found.
questions.explanation: India has deposits of high-grade iron ore. The mineral is found mainly in Jharkhand, Orissa, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Goa, Maharashtra, and Karnataka. It is not found in Gujarat.

questions.question: From the options, select the major bauxite producing area of India.
questions.explanation: Major bauxite producing areas are Jharkhand, Orissa, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Name the country which is the largest producer and exporter of Mica in the world.
questions.explanation: Mica deposits mainly occur in Jharkhand, Bihar, Andhra Pradesh, and Rajasthan. India is the largest producer and exporter of mica in the world.

questions.question: Which state among the following does not have copper deposits in India?
questions.explanation: Copper deposits are mainly produced in Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Jharkhand, Karnataka, and Andhra Pradesh. It is not present in Kerela.

questions.question: Name the important mineral, which is used as a raw material for the smelting of iron ore.
questions.explanation: For smelting iron ore, manganese is used.

questions.question: Choose the mineral used as a raw material for the cement industry:
questions.explanation: The major raw material for cement making is usually limestone.

questions.question: Identify this place marked A, in Karnataka, famous for deposits of gold.
questions.explanation: The place in the given map is Kolar. Kolar Gold Fields is one of the major gold mines in India and is located in the Kolar district in Karnataka.

questions.question: Analyse the map of India and identify the place marked B, an important place in Tamil Nadu where there is rich source of salt available.
questions.explanation: The given place in map is Tuticorin in Tamilnadu. Being a port with dry weather, Tuticorin is highly suitable for salt production.

questions.question: Extraction of Salt from a famous place in Rajasthan is given below. Identify the place.
questions.explanation: Sambhar Lake in Rajasthan is India’s the biggest saline lake. The Sambhar Salt Lake is the biggest inland salt lake in India

questions.question:
Which of the following sectors depend on power for their development?

  1. Industry
  2. Agriculture
  3. Transport
  4. Communication
  5. Defense
questions.explanation: Power or energy plays a vital role in our lives. We also need power for industry, agriculture, transport, communication, and defence.

questions.question: Name the term given to the Power resources like Firewood, Fossil fuels, etc. that have been in common use for a long time.
questions.explanation: Conventional sources of energy are those which have been in common use for a long time. Firewood and fossil fuels are the two main conventional energy sources.

questions.question:
Identify this Conventional source of energy based on the clue given:

  1. This is the main source of fuel in India today.
  2. Most of the people living in under-developed countries use this for domestic cooking and for heating.
questions.explanation: Firewood is widely used for cooking and heating. In our country, more than fifty percent of the energy used by villagers comes from firewood.

questions.question: Remains of plants and animals which were buried under the earth for millions of years got converted by the heat and pressure. Name the term given to these conventional sources of energy.
questions.explanation: Conventional sources of energy are those which have been in common use for a long time. Fossil fuels are the main conventional energy sources. Remains of plants and animals which were buried under the earth for millions of years got converted by the heat and pressure into fossil fuels.

questions.question: Which one of the following cannot be listed under Fossil Fuels?
questions.explanation: Fossil fuels such as coal, petroleum, and natural gas are the main sources of conventional energy. Firewood is not a fossil fuel.

questions.question: Select the most abundant found fossil fuel.
questions.explanation: Fossil fuels such as coal, petroleum, and natural gas are the main sources of conventional energy. Among them, Coal is the most abundantly found fossil fuel.

questions.question:
Identify the name of this convenient source of energy based on the clues given:

  1. This word is derived from the Latin words Petra meaning rock and Oleum meaning oil.
  2. This is found between the layers of rocks and is drilled from oil fields located in off-shore and coastal areas.
questions.explanation: The word petroleum is derived from Latin words-Petra meaning rock, oleum meaning oil. So, petroleum means rock oil. The petrol that keeps your car running as well as the oil that keeps your cycle from squeaking, both began as a thick black liquid called Petroleum. It is found between the layers of rocks and is drilled from oil fields located in off-shore and coastal areas.

questions.question: Name the type of electricity generated from coal.
questions.explanation: Coal is used as a domestic fuel, in industries such as iron and steel, steam engines and to generate electricity. Electricity from coal is called thermal power.

questions.question:
From the following reasons, pick out the one as to why Coal is referred to as Buried Sunshine.

  1. Coal is used to generate thermal electricity.
  2. Coal was formed millions of years ago when giant ferns and swamps got buried under the layers of earth.
  3. Coal is used as a domestic fuel in the iron and steel industry and also in steam engines.
  4. Coal is the most abundantly found fossil fuel.
questions.explanation: Coal is the most abundantly found fossil fuel. The coal which we are using today was formed millions of years ago when giant ferns and swamps got buried under the layers of earth. Coal is therefore referred to as Buried Sunshine.

questions.question: Pick out the country that is not a leading producer of Coal in the world.
questions.explanation: The leading coal producers of the world are China, the USA, Germany, Russia, South Africa, and France. Pakistan is not included in this list.

questions.question: Select the important state in India producing Coal.
questions.explanation: The coal-producing areas of India are Raniganj, Jharia, Dhanbad, and Bokaro in Jharkhand.

questions.question: Name the major Coal producing area in India which is situated in the Asansol subdivision of West Bengal in India.
questions.explanation: Raniganj, the city in West Bengal is one of the coal-producing areas of India.

questions.question: Which one of the following, situated in Assam is a leading producer of Petroleum in India?
questions.explanation: Digboi in Assam is an oil town where oil was first discovered and is the leading producer of Petroleum in India.

questions.question: Select the correct reason from the following options as to why Petroleum is called Black gold or Liquid gold?

options.html: As the mineral oil is taken from the sedimentary rocks
More than half of total reserves are found in Middle East countries

questions.explanation: Petroleum and its derivatives are called Black Gold as they are very valuable.

questions.question: Pick out the country that is not a leading producer of Natural Gas.
questions.explanation: Russia, Norway, UK, and the Netherlands are the major producers of natural gas. It is not found in Chile.

questions.question: Name the largest producer as well as the consumer of Natural Gas in the world.
questions.explanation: The USA leads in production and consumption of Natural Gas.

questions.question:
A few steps to generate power are given below:

  1. Rainwater or river water stored in dams is made to fall from heights.
  2. The falling water flows through pipes inside the dam over turbine blades placed at the bottom of the dam.
  3. The moving blades then turn the generator to produce electricity.

Name the electricity generated with the help of these clues.
questions.explanation: Rainwater or river water stored in dams is made to fall from heights. The falling water flows through pipes inside the dam over turbine blades placed at the bottom of the dam. The moving blades then turn the generator to produce electricity. This is called hydroelectricity. The water discharged after the generation of electricity is used for irrigation.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not an important hydel power station in India?
questions.explanation: Some important hydel power stations in India are Bhakra Nangal, Gandhi Sagar, Nagarjunsagar, and Damodar valley projects. Nuclear power is located in Tarapur.

questions.question: Pick the one that is not a non-conventional source of energy.
questions.explanation: Examples of non-conventional sources are solar energy, wind energy, tidal energy which are renewable. Hydro-Electric Power is not a non-conventional source of energy.

questions.question: Identify the non- conventional source of energy generated from the image given
questions.explanation: Solar energy trapped from the sun can be used in solar cells to produce electricity. The given image is of Solar Panels to trap solar energy.

questions.question: Identify the type of energy generated. It is pollution free, inexhaustible source of energy. In this the kinetic energy of wind is converted into electrical energy through turbines. This was used for grinding grain and lifting water since times immemorial.
questions.explanation:
In modern time wind mills, the high speed 
winds rotate the wind mill which is connected to a generator to produce electricity.

questions.question: A list of countries popular for their wind farms is given below. Pick out the one where the wind farm is not established.
questions.explanation: Windfarms are found in Netherlands, Germany, Denmark, UK, USA, and Spain are noted for their wind energy production. It has not been established in South Africa.

questions.question: The wind power plant at Lamba in India is the largest in Asia. Name the state where Lamba is located.
questions.explanation: Lamba in Gujarat is famous for wind power plants. It has the largest windmill in Asia.

questions.question: Name the important radioactive elements linked to the production of Nuclear Power.
questions.explanation: Nuclear power is obtained from energy stored in the nuclei of atoms of naturally occurring radioactive elements like uranium and thorium.

questions.question: Energy is produced when the nucleus splits into two or more smaller nuclei. Name this process.
questions.explanation: Nuclear power is obtained from energy stored in the nuclei of atoms of naturally occurring radioactive elements like uranium and thorium. These fuels undergo nuclear fission i.e the nucleus splits into two or more smaller nuclei, in nuclear reactors, and emit power.

questions.question: Mention the year in which the first nuclear plant was set up in the UK.
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom established the world's first civil nuclear programme, opening a nuclear power station, Calder Hall at Windscale, England, in 1956.

questions.question: The greatest nuclear power producers are Europe and ________.
questions.explanation: The greatest producers of nuclear power are USA and Europe.

questions.question: Name the two important states where large deposits of Uranium are found.
questions.explanation: Rajasthan and Jharkhand are rich in Uranium reserves.

questions.question: Thorium is found in large quantities in the Monazite sands of an important state of India. From the options given, choose the state.
questions.explanation: Thorium is found in large quantities in the Monazite sands of Kerala.

questions.question: An important process is represented by the image given below. Identify the type of energy generated
questions.explanation: The given process explains Nuclear power is obtained from energy stored in the nuclei of atoms of naturally occurring radio active elements like uranium and thorium

questions.question: From the map of India, identify the place in Tamilnadu, where an important nuclear power station is located.
questions.explanation: The given nuclear power station is in India located in Kalpakkam in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Name the Indian State where the nuclear power station Tarapur is located.
questions.explanation: The nuclear power stations in India is located at Tarapur in Maharastra.

questions.question: Name the energy obtained from the earth used for cooking heating and bathing .
questions.explanation: Geothermal  energy in the form of hot springs has been used for cooking, heating and bathing for several years.

questions.question: Name the country with the world's largest geothermal power plant.
questions.explanation: The USA has the world’s largest geothermal power plants followed by New Zealand, Iceland, Philippines, and Central America.

questions.question: Name the important geothermal power plants located in India.
questions.explanation: In India, geothermal plants are located in Manikaran in Himachal Pradesh and Puga Valley in Ladakh.

questions.question: A dam is used to trap water at high tide and released at the time of low tide. The plant rising and falling water can run generators and produce electricity. Name this important source of energy generated.
questions.explanation: Energy generated from tides is called tidal energy. Tidal energy can be harnessed by building dams at narrow openings of the sea. During high tide, the energy of the tides is used to turn the turbine installed in the dam to produce electricity.

questions.question: Analyse the image given below and from the list of options, pick out the apt one to name the type of gaseous fuel generated.
questions.explanation: Organic waste such as dead plant and animal material, animal dung and kitchen waste can be converted into a gaseous fuel called BioGas.

questions.question: Biogas digesters emit biogas which is essentially a mixture of two components. Name them:
questions.explanation: Organic waste is decomposed by bacteria in biogas digesters to emit biogas which is essentially a mixture of methane and carbon dioxide.

questions.question:
A few points pertaining to the Non-Conventional Sources of Energy are given below. Pick out the one that is not correct.

  1. The expenses to set up Wind energy and Solar energy plants are very less when compared to setting up of Tidal Energy plant.
  2. Nuclear energy generates radioactive waste and is also very expensive to set up.
  3. Bio Gas is very effective and can be done at low cost.
  4. Geothermal energy is eco-friendly and always available.
questions.explanation: The expenses to set up Wind energy and Solar energy plants are relatively high when compared to setting up of Tidal Energy plant.

questions.question: Energy is everywhere but we can see that harnessing this energy is both difficult and costly. How can each one of us make a difference? Pick out the most appropriate one with respect to the above statement.

options.html: By saving energy and not wasting
By making use of non-polluting vehicles
By making use of biogas only

questions.explanation: By Optimum usage, without any wastage and saving of the energy.

questions.question:
From the given options, which one is the appropriate definition for the term Agriculture?

  1. The Process of preparing the land for the cultivation of crops, and also to rear livestock.
  2. The size of landholdings in an area.
  3. The temperature and the amount of rainfall required for certain crops to grow.
  4. The extraction and production of resources by nature.
questions.explanation: The word agriculture is derived from Latin words ager or Agri meaning soil and culture meaning, cultivation, or tilling of the soil. Agriculture is a primary activity. It includes growing crops, fruits, vegetables, flowers, and rearing of livestock.

questions.question: Breeding of fish in specially constructed tanks and ponds is known as:
questions.explanation: Fish farming or pisciculture involves raising fish commercially in tanks or enclosures such as fish ponds, usually for food.

questions.question: Name the type of economic activities.
questions.explanation: The transformation from a plant to a finished product involves three types of economic activities. These are primary, secondary, and tertiary activities. Primary activities include all those connected with the extraction and production of natural resources. Agriculture, fishing, and gathering are good examples. Secondary activities are concerned with the processing of these resources. Tertiary activities provide support to the primary and secondary sectors through services. Transport, trade, banking, insurance, and advertising are examples of tertiary activities.

questions.question: Name the type of activity that includes all those connected with the extraction and production of natural resources.
questions.explanation: Primary activities include all those connected with the extraction and production of natural resources. Agriculture, fishing, and gathering are good examples of primary activities.

questions.question: A few examples of primary activities are given below. Pick out the one that is not a primary activity.
questions.explanation: Secondary activities are concerned with the processing of resources. Manufacturing of steel, baking of bread, and weaving of cloth are examples of this activity.

questions.question: From the following, which one best describes the Secondary Economic activities?

options.html: Activities involving a large human population yielding good turnover
Activities involving the processing of natural resources
Activities that help the primary and secondary activities
Extraction and production of natural resources

questions.explanation:
There are three types of economic activities: primary, secondary and tertiary activities.

Primary activities include all those connected with the extraction and production of natural resources. For e.g. agriculture, fishing, and gathering. Secondary activities are concerned with the processing of these natural resources, like manufacturing of steel, baking of bread, and weaving of cloth are examples of this activity.

questions.question: Pick out the example of secondary economic activity from the given list of options.
questions.explanation: Secondary activities are concerned with the processing of resources. Manufacturing of steel, baking of bread, and weaving of cloth are examples of this activity.

questions.question: Banking, Insurance sectors, etc can be a good example of which of the following type of economic activity?
questions.explanation: Tertiary activities provide support to the primary and secondary sectors through services. Transport, trade, banking, insurance, and advertising are examples of tertiary activities.

questions.question: Pick out the approximate percentage of the world's population engaged in agriculture.
questions.explanation: The percentage of the world's population engaged in agriculture has decreased over time due to industrialization and urbanization. According to global estimates, approximately 25-30% of the labor force is engaged in agriculture. Among the given options, 30% is the closest approximation. Options like 50% or 70% overstate the current global figure, while 10% is too low.

questions.question: Name the term given to the land on which the crops are grown.
questions.explanation: Agriculture is a primary activity. It includes growing crops, fruits, vegetables, flowers, and rearing of livestock. Favourable topography of soil and climate is vital for agricultural activity. The land on which the crops are grown is known as arable land.

questions.question: Name the term given to the commercial rearing of silkworms.
questions.explanation: Sericulture is the commercial rearing of silkworms. It may supplement the income of the farmer.

questions.question: Name the term given to the Breeding of Fish in specially constructed tanks and ponds.
questions.explanation: Fish farming or pisciculture involves raising fish commercially in tanks or enclosures such as fish ponds, usually for food. It is the principal form of aquaculture, while other methods may fall under mariculture. Worldwide, the most important fish species produced in fish farming are carp, tilapia, salmon, and catfish.

questions.question: Name the term given to the Cultivation of grapes.
questions.explanation: Viticulture is the science, production, and study of grapes. It deals with the series of events that occur in the vineyard. It is a branch of the science of horticulture.

questions.question: Name the term given to Growing of Vegetables flowers and fruits for commercial use.
questions.explanation: Horticulture is the science and art of growing (plants) - fruits, vegetables, flowers, and any other cultivar. It also includes plant conservation, landscape restoration, soil management, landscape and garden design, construction, and maintenance.

questions.question: Agriculture or farming can be looked at as a system. Which one among the following is not an important input of agriculture?
questions.explanation: Agriculture or farming can be looked at as a system. The important inputs are seeds, fertilisers, machinery, and labour. Some of the operations involved are ploughing, sowing, irrigation, weeding, and harvesting. The outputs from the system include crops, wool, dairy, and poultry products.

questions.question: Which one among the following is not an important output of agriculture?
questions.explanation: Agriculture or farming can be looked at as a system. The important inputs are seeds, fertilisers, machinery, and labour. Some of the operations involved are ploughing, sowing, irrigation, weeding, and harvesting. The outputs from the system include crops, wool, dairy, and poultry products.

questions.question: What does the word agri mean?
questions.explanation: The word agriculture is derived from Latin words ager or agri meaning soil and culture meaning cultivation, or tilling of the soil.

questions.question: Analyse the following and identify which one among the following can be treated as Inputs to the agriculture system.
questions.explanation: Agriculture or farming can be looked at as a system. The important inputs are seeds, fertilisers, machinery and labour. Some of the operations involved are ploughing, sowing, irrigation, weeding and harvesting. The outputs from the system include crops, wool, dairy and poultry products.

questions.question: Mention the processing part of the agriculture system
questions.explanation: Agriculture or farming can be looked at as a system. The important inputs are seeds, fertilisers, machinery and labour. Some of the operations involved are ploughing, sowing, irrigation, weeding and harvesting. The outputs from the system include crops, wool, dairy and poultry products.

questions.question: Which one from the following list is not a Human Input to the agriculture system?
questions.explanation: Agriculture or farming can be looked at as a system. The important inputs are seeds, fertilisers, machinery, and labour. Some of the operations involved are ploughing, sowing, irrigation, weeding, and harvesting. Rainfall, on the other hand, is a physical input.

questions.question: From the following list of inputs, mention the one that is not a natural input in the agriculture system.
questions.explanation: Pesticides are substances that are meant to control pests (including weeds). In general, a pesticide is a chemical or biological agent that deters, incapacitates, kills, or otherwise discourages pests.

questions.question: Name the type of farming practiced to meet the needs of the farmer's family?
questions.explanation: Subsistence Farming is practised to meet the needs of the farmer’s family. Traditionally, low levels of technology and household labour are used to produce on small output. Subsistence farming can be further classified as intensive subsistence and primitive subsistence farming.

questions.question: Fill up the missing blank after analyzing the given image:
questions.explanation: Subsistence Farming is practised to meet the needs of the farmer’s family. Traditionally, low levels of technology and household labour are used to produce on small output. Subsistence farming can be further classified as intensive subsistence and primitive subsistence farming.

questions.question:
Which one of the following best describes intensive subsistence agriculture?

  1. A plot of land is cleared by felling the trees and burning them in thickly populated forested areas.
  2. The farmer cultivates a small plot of land using simple tools and more labour.
  3. Crops are grown and animals are reared for sale in the market.
  4. Herdsmen move from place to place with their animals for fodder and water along defined routes.
questions.explanation: In intensive subsistence agriculture, the farmer cultivates a small plot of land using simple tools and more labour. Climate with a large number of days with sunshine and fertile soil permit growing of more than one crop annually on the same plot.

questions.question: A few statements about Intensive Subsistence agriculture are given below. Pick out the one that is not applicable.

options.html: Bright sunny days with and fertile soils aides in growing more than one crop annually
A plot of land is cleared by felling the trees and burning
It is prevalent in the monsoon regions of south, southeast, and east Asia

questions.explanation: In intensive subsistence agriculture, the farmer cultivates a small plot using simple tools and more labour. Climate with a large number of days with sunshine and fertile soil permits the growing of more than one crop annually on the same plot. Rice is the main crop. Other crops include wheat, maize, pulses, and oilseeds. Intensive subsistence agriculture is prevalent in the thickly populated areas of the monsoon regions of the south, southeast, and east Asia.

questions.question: Which is true regarding shifting cultivation:

options.html: It is also known as ‘slash and burn’ agriculture
The cultivator own the plot and stays there

questions.explanation: Shifting cultivation is practised in the thickly forested areas of the Amazon basin, tropical Africa, parts of Southeast Asia, and Northeast India. A plot of land is cleared by felling the trees and burning them. The ashes are then mixed with the soil and crops like maize, yam, potatoes, and cassava are grown. After the soil loses its fertility, the land is abandoned and the cultivator moves to a new plot. Shifting cultivation is also known as ‘slash and burn’ agriculture.

questions.question: This type of farming is practiced in the arid and semi-arid regions of Sahara, Central Asia, and Rajasthan, etc. Herdsmen move from place to place with their animals for fodder and water along defined routes due to climatic constraints and terrain. Which type of farming is being referred to?
questions.explanation: Nomadic herding is practised in the semi-arid and arid regions of Sahara, Central Asia, and some parts of India, like Rajasthan and Jammu and Kashmir. In this type of farming, herdsmen move from place to place with their animals for fodder and water, along with defined routes. This type of movement arises in response to climatic constraints and terrain. Sheep, camel, yak, and goats are most commonly reared. They provide milk, meat, wool, hides, and other products to the herders and their families.

questions.question: Name the type of farming followed from the given image
questions.explanation: Nomadic herding is practised in the semi-arid and arid regions of Sahara, Central Asia and some parts of India, like Rajasthan and Jammu and Kashmir. In this type of farming, herdsmen move from place to place with their animals for fodder and water, along defined routes. This type of movement arises in response to climatic constraints and terrain. Sheep, camel, yak and goats are most commonly reared. They provide milk, meat, wool, hides and other products to the herders and their families

questions.question: Which of the following animals is not reared by the nomadic herders?
questions.explanation: Nomadic herding is practised in the semi-arid and arid regions of Sahara, Central Asia, and some parts of India, like Rajasthan and Jammu and Kashmir. In this type of farming, herdsmen move from place to place with their animals for fodder and water, along with defined routes. Sheep, camel, yak and goats are most commonly reared. They provide milk, meat, wool, hides, and other products to the herders and their families.

questions.question: The main motive of this type of cultivation is profit. A large area of land and a huge amount of capital is used for cultivation with the latest technological advancement. Name the type of farming from the given list.
questions.explanation: In commercial farming crops are grown and animals are reared for sale in the market. The area cultivated and the amount of capital used is large. Most of the work is done by machines. Commercial farming includes commercial grain farming, mixed farming, and plantation agriculture. In commercial grain farming crops are grown for commercial purposes. In mixed farming, the land is used for growing food and fodder crops and rearing livestock. Plantations are a type of commercial farming where a single crop of tea, coffee, sugarcane, cashew, rubber, banana or cotton are grown.

questions.question: This type of cultivation was introduced by the Europeans in tropical regions. Here vast lands are brought under a single crop with large amounts of capital, cheap but skilled labour, scientific techniques, good transport network and good managerial and marketing skills. .The attached image shows tea cultivation in India, name the type of cultivation used
questions.explanation: Plantations are a type of commercial farming where single crop of tea, coffee, sugarcane, cashew, rubber, banana or cotton are grown. Large amount of labour and capital are required. The produce may be processed on the farm itself or in nearby factories. The development of a transport network is thus essential for such farming.

questions.question: A large variety of crops are grown to meet the requirement of the growing population. Identify this major crop of the world that is also the staple diet of the tropical and sub-tropical regions.
questions.explanation: Rice is the major food crop of the world. It is the staple diet of the tropical and sub-tropical regions. Rice needs high temperature, high humidity and rainfall. It grows best in alluvial clayey soil, which can retain water. China leads in the production of rice followed by India, Japan, Sri Lanka and Egypt. In favourable climatic conditions as in West Bengal and Bangladesh two to three crops a year are grown.

questions.question: This crop requires moderate temperature and rainfall during growing season and bright sunshine at the time of harvest. Also thrives well in loamy soil. In India, Punjab is one of the important pockets of this crop. Which is this crop?
questions.explanation: Wheat requires moderate temperature and rainfall during growing season and bright sunshine at the time of harvest. It thrives best in well drained loamy soil. Wheat is grown extensively in USA, Canada, Argentina, Russia, Ukraine, Australia and India. In India it is grown in winter.

questions.question: Known as Coarse grains this crop can be grown on less fertile and sandy soils. Requiring low rainfall and high to moderate temperature, this is grown in India and other countries like Nigeria, China and Niger. With the help of the picture given below, name the crop.
questions.explanation: Millets are also known as coarse grains and can be grown on less fertile and sandy soils. It is a hardy crop that needs low rainfall and high to moderate temperature and adequate rainfall. Jowar, bajra and ragi are grown in India. Other countries are Nigeria, China and Niger.

questions.question: Name the crop that is also known as Paddy?
questions.explanation: Rice is the major food crop in the world. It is also known as paddy. It is the staple diet of the tropical and sub-tropical regions. Rice needs high temperatures, high humidity, and rainfall. It grows best in alluvial clayey soil, which can retain water. China leads in the production of rice followed by India, Japan, Sri Lanka, and Egypt.

questions.question: Name the country that is the leading producer of Rice in the world.
questions.explanation: Rice is the major food crop in the world. It is the staple diet of the tropical and sub-tropical regions. Rice needs high temperatures, high humidity, and rainfall. It grows best in alluvial clayey soil, which can retain water. China leads in the production of rice followed by India, Japan, Sri Lanka, and Egypt. In favourable climatic conditions as in West Bengal and Bangladesh two to three crops, a year are grown.

questions.question: Which among the following are fibre crops?
questions.explanation:
Cotton requires high temperature, light rainfall, two hundred and ten frost-free days, and bright sunshine for its growth. It grows best on black and alluvial soils. China, USA, India, Pakistan, Brazil, and Egypt are the leading producers of cotton. It is one of the main raw materials for the cotton textile industry.
Jute was also known as the ‘Golden Fibre’. It grows well on alluvial soil and requires high temperature, heavy rainfall, and humid climate. This crop is grown in tropical areas. India and Bangladesh are the leading producers of jute.

questions.question: The major raw material for the textile industry, India is a leading producer of this crop. Requires high temperature, light rainfall, two hundred and ten frost-free days, and bright sunshine for its growth, this grows best on black and alluvial soil. Name the crop.
questions.explanation: Cotton requires high temperature, light rainfall, two hundred and ten frost-free days, and bright sunshine for its growth. It grows best on black and alluvial soils. China, USA, India, Pakistan, Brazil, and Egypt are the leading producers of cotton. It is one of the main raw materials for the cotton textile industry.

questions.question: Which crop is also known as the Golden Fibre.
questions.explanation: Jute was also known as the ‘Golden Fibre’. It grows well on alluvial soil and requires high temperature, heavy rainfall, and humid climate. This crop is grown in tropical areas. India and Bangladesh are the leading producers of jute.

questions.question: Coffee requires a warm and wet climate and well-drained loamy soil. Name the country that is the leading producer of Coffee.
questions.explanation: Coffee requires warm and wet climate and well-drained loamy soil. Hill slopes are more suitable for the growth of this crop. Brazil is the leading producer of coffee followed by Columbia and India.

questions.question: A few conditions with respect to the growth of Tea are given below. Pick out the one that is apt to Coffee.

options.html: Requires a warm and wet climate and well-drained loamy soil
Needs well-drained loamy soils and gentle slopes
Labour in large number is required to pick the leaves

questions.explanation: Coffee requires a warm and wet climate and well-drained loamy soil. Hill slopes are more suitable for the growth of this crop. Whereas, tea requires a cool climate and well-distributed high rainfall throughout the year for the growth of its tender leaves. It needs well-drained loamy soils and gentle slopes. Labour in large numbers is required to pick the leaves.

questions.question: This crop is grown for its tender leaves. India is one of the leading producers of world, and 70% is consumed within India itself. Shown below is an important garden in Darjeeling famous for this crop. Identify this crop
questions.explanation: Tea: Tea is a beverage crop grown on plantations. This requires cool climate and well distributed high rainfall throughout the year for the growth of its tender leaves.It needs well-drained loamy soils and gentle slopes. Labour in large number is required to pick the leaves. Kenya, India, China, Sri Lanka produce the best quality tea in the world.

questions.question: The efforts made to increase farm production in order to meet the growing demand of the increasing population is called ________.
questions.explanation: Agricultural Development refers to efforts made to increase farm production in order to meet the growing demand of the increasing population. This can be achieved in many ways such as increasing the cropped area, the number of crops grown, improving irrigation facilities, the use of fertilisers and high yielding variety of seeds. The mechanization of agriculture is also another aspect of agricultural development.

questions.question:
Listed below are a few practices to adopt for sustainable agricultural development. Which of these statements are correct and effective to implement?

  1. Improving irrigation facilities
  2. Use of fertilisers
  3. Use of High yielding varieties of seeds
questions.explanation: Agricultural Development refers to efforts made to increase farm production in order to meet the growing demand of the increasing population. This can be achieved in many ways such as increasing the cropped area, the number of crops grown, improving irrigation facilities, the use of fertilizers, and high yielding variety of seeds. The mechanization of agriculture is also another aspect of agricultural development. The ultimate aim of agricultural development is to increase food security.

questions.question:
A few statements with respect to the farm in the USA are given below. Pick out the one that is not true.

  1. The average size of a farm in the USA is much larger that of an Indian farm ie about 25 hectares.
  2. The farmer generally resides on the farm.
  3. The farmer does not store the produce and sells even when the markets are not favourable to them.
  4. The farmer from time to time sends soil samples to the laboratory to check the nutrients.
questions.explanation: The average size of a farm in the USA is much larger than that of an Indian farm. The typical farm size in the USA is about 250 hectares. The farmer generally resides in the farm. Some of the major crops grown are corn, soybeans, wheat, cotton, and sugarbeet. The farmer in USA works like a businessman and not like a peasant farmer.

questions.question: Which one among the following best describes an economic activity that is concerned with the production of goods, extraction of minerals, or the provision of services?
questions.explanation: It is because industries are the one which changes the raw material into the product of more value to the people.

questions.question: Complete the following based on the representation: Generally Industries can be classified on the basis of raw materials, __________ and ownership.
questions.explanation: Classification of industries on the basis of size depends upon the: amount of capital invested, number of people employed and the volume of production.

questions.question: Which of the following can be considered as an example of the economic activity related to the production of goods from the industry sector?
questions.explanation: Because new goods are produced out of raw materials like iron, steel, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following can be an example of the economic activity related to the extraction of minerals from the industry sector?
questions.explanation: Because extracting the coal requires a process that is followed by the industries to extract the raw material.

questions.question: Which of the following can be considered as an example of the service provider economic activity from the industry sector?
questions.explanation: Banking falls under the tertiary sector of the economy and it provides banking and its allied services to the people in day to day life.

questions.question: Classify the industries based on the Raw materials they use and complete the image given below:
questions.explanation: Extracted minerals are used widely as raw materials in various manufacturing industries like iron & steel, locomotives etc.,

questions.question:
Which of the following best describes an agro-based industry?

  1. Industries depending on agricultural products Ex-plant and animal products.
  2. Industries depending on ores like Cement, iron, and steel, etc.
  3. Industries that use products from the sea and oceans as raw materials.
  4. Industries that depend upon the primary products from forests for their raw materials.
questions.explanation: Industries which derive its raw materials from animals and plant products are termed as agro-based industries.

questions.question: To which category of industry (based on raw materials) does Iron made from iron ore belong to?
questions.explanation: Because iron is metal which is obtained after extraction of iron ore(mineral) from the earth.

questions.question: Processing seafood and manufacturing fish oil can be considered as examples of:
questions.explanation: Because seafood and fish oil can be obtained in the areas with seas, oceans, etc., which supports marine life. Hence, it falls under Marine Based Industries.

questions.question: Pulp and Paper, Pharmaceuticals, Furniture, Buildings, etc are good examples of:
questions.explanation: Because the raw materials used in these industries are obtained from the forest region (dense trees). Hence it falls under Forest-Based Industries.

questions.question:
Which of the following is true with respect to the classification of industries on the basis of size:

  1. Based on the capital invested by the Company
  2. Based on the total number of employees
  3. Based on the volume of production
questions.explanation: Based on the size, industries can be classified into Large Scale Industries and Small Scale Industries. Capital Invested, the total number of employees and the volume of the production are the three criteria used to classify the industries.

questions.question: Which industries are also known as a village or household industries?
questions.explanation: They are called so because they are small scale industries with less capital and hand made goods.

questions.question: Basket weaving, pottery, and other handicrafts are all examples of:
questions.explanation: Because, these goods do not require a large scale industry and a huge number of employees and moreover, these goods are hand-made.

questions.question: With limited capital, a food processing unit is set up in a particular village in Gujarat. This unit generates employment to that particular village. Name the type of classification of the industry under which this food processing unit will come under.
questions.explanation: It is because the industry is set up with limited capital and as mentioned the industry generates employment only to the people of that particular village.

questions.question:
This particular classification of the industry requires: 

  1. large amounts of capital,
  2. huge machines and modern and superior technology to manufacture goods,
  3. employing a huge number of employees. 

For eg: iron and steel industry, the production is on a mass scale and is voluminous. Mention the type of industry to which this classification belong to:
questions.explanation: As it is the classification based on the size, it includes Huge capital investment, more number of employees, and a high volume of production.

questions.question: The following image shows an important Rail Coach factory in Kapurthala. Identify the classification of the industry based on its size.
questions.explanation: Because, manufacturing of rail coach involves setting up of a huge industry with a high capital and employment as it draws its raw material from iron and steel industry which itself is a large scale industry.

questions.question: Complete the following : On the basis of Ownership, industries can be classified as :
questions.explanation: Co-operative sector industries are owned and operated by the producers or suppliers of raw materials, workers or both.

questions.question: ________ sector industries are owned, managed, and controlled by the state or central government.
questions.explanation: Because the public sector industries are owned and operated by the government.

questions.question: The industries like Indian railways, Hindustan Aeronautics Limited, etc., operate more for the benefit of the public rather than for making a profit and the price for their products is fixed through planning. Name the sector to which these industries belong to_______.
questions.explanation: Because these industries are owned and operated by the government.

questions.question: The leading industries of Birla Group, Reliance, Tata Group of industries belong to the private sector. Choose the characteristic features of these industries?

options.html: Members of the cooperative society produce raw material and add value to earn a profit. Generally operates with the motive of community welfare
Operated mainly for the benefit of the public rather than profits, they are owned, managed, and controlled by a group of individuals. Prices of products are determined by market forces
Jointly owned, they are owned, managed, and controlled by a group of individuals and government agencies. Prices of products are fixed through planning
Operated mainly for Profits, they are owned, managed and controlled by a group of individuals. Prices of products are determined by market forces.

questions.explanation: As the above-mentioned industries come under the ownership of the private sector industries owned by a group of private individuals. Therefore, such industries aim for profit-making and fix the prices based on market forces.

questions.question: Joint sector Industries are owned and operated by the state and individual or a group of individuals. From the given list of industries, which one can be called a Joint Sector industry?
questions.explanation: Because Maruti Udyog Limited is owned and operated by the state and individual or a group of individuals.

questions.question: The following image is that of Anand Milk Union Limited (AMUL), situated in the state of Gujarat. To which industry type does this venture that links 3 million milk producers of Gujarat come under?
questions.explanation: Because, Anand Milk Union Limited (AMUL) is owned and operated by the producers of milk and suppliers of milk i.e. Sudha Dairy. So, it comes under Co-operative Sector.

questions.question: A few factors affecting the location of industries are given below. Complete the list, by choosing the one that can be added to this list.
questions.explanation: Labour is one of the most important of the four factors of production of any economy.  Location of any industry can impact the number of employees/labour that can be appointed or employeed in a particular industry from that particular area.

questions.question: An industrial system consists of inputs, processes, and outputs. A list of items with respect to the textile industry is given below. Identify the one which cannot be classified under inputs.
questions.explanation: Weaving is not raw material or input but it is the process that converts raw material into a semi-finished or finished product. Hence, it cannot be considered as an input.

questions.question: An industrial system related to the Bakery industry is given below. Which of the following options gives the right data?

options.html:
INPUT: Bread
PROCESSING: Wheat flour, Power, Ghee, Oil
OUTPUT: Baking

INPUT: Baking
PROCESSING: Bread
OUTPUT: Wheat flour, Power, Ghee, Oil

INPUT: Wheat flour, Power, Ghee, Oil
PROCESSING: Bread
OUTPUT: Baking

INPUT: Wheat flour, Power, Ghee, Oil
PROCESSING: Baking
OUTPUT: Bread

questions.explanation: It is the process intending to produce bread as its final product. Hence, Wheat flour, Ghee, Oil, and Power are the inputs. The process involved in Baking and the output produced is bread.

questions.question: In the light of various factors that influence industrial location, some industries have a tendency to grow close to each other. Name such areas.
questions.explanation: Industrial regions emerge when a number of industries locate close to each other and share the benefits of their closeness. Major industrial regions of the world are eastern North America, western and central Europe, eastern Europe, and eastern Asia. India has several industrial regions like Mumbai-Pune cluster, Bangalore-Tamil Nadu region, Hugli region, Ahmedabad-Baroda region, Chottanagpur industrial belt Vishakhapatnam-Guntur belt, Gurgaon-Delhi-Meerut region and the Kollam-Thiruvanathapuram industrial cluster.

questions.question: Name the three important industrial regions of the world.

options.html: Eastern North America, Western and Central Europe, Western Australia
Eastern North America, Western and Central Europe, Eastern Asia
Eastern Australia, Western and Central Europe, Eastern Asia

questions.explanation: Major industrial regions of the world are eastern North America, western and central Europe, eastern Europe, and eastern Asia.

questions.question: Which one among the following is not an industrial region of India?
questions.explanation: India has several industrial regions like the Mumbai-Pune cluster, Bangalore-Tamil Nadu region, Hugli region, Ahmedabad-Baroda region, Chottanagpur industrial belt, Vishakhapatnam-Guntur belt, Gurgaon-Delhi-Meerut region and the Kollam-Thiruvanathapuram industrial cluster.

questions.question: Name the important Western industrial region in India.
questions.explanation: Ahmedabad and Vadodara are the cities in Gujarat, which lies in western India and are a major industrial region.

questions.question: Name the important Northern industrial region in India.
questions.explanation: Because, Gurgaon, Delhi, and Meerut lie in the northern part of India.

tests.name: Industries Test 03
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Social Science class 08 Social Science Geography Industries CBSE Online Test 03 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which one among the following is an important industrial region of South India?
questions.explanation: Because this region witnessed the most rapid industrial growth in the post-Independence period. Bangalore is the capital of Karnataka and both Karnataka and Tamil Nadu lies in Southern India.

questions.question: Which industry is considered as the key or basic industry?
questions.explanation: Because, All industries whether heavy, medium, or light depend on it for the machinery. Thus, it provides the base to the other industries. Steel is required for manufacturing a variety of engineering, construction and defence goods.

questions.question: Which one among the following is termed as emerging industry?
questions.explanation: Because it is in the phase of growth and is expected to give vast exposure to the people globally.

questions.question: Emerging industries can also be named as:
questions.explanation: A sunrise industry is one that is new or relatively new, is growing fast and is expected to become important in the future.

questions.question: From the list given below, which country does not have an iron and steel industry located?
questions.explanation: The countries in which the iron and steel industry is located are Germany, USA, China, Japan, and Russia.

questions.question: Many countries have a hub of the Textile industry. From the list given below, choose the country that does not have a textile industry?
questions.explanation: In Germany, Iron and Steel Industries are located.

questions.question:
Why is the iron and steel industry considered as a feeder industry?

  1. Usually, Iron and steel plants are located near coal mines.
  2. Products are used as raw materials for other industries.
  3. Usually, iron and steel plants are located where there is an abundant supply of water and power.
  4. Steel is tough and strong.
questions.explanation: Because its products are used as raw material for other industries. For example steel.

questions.question:
Analyse the image given below and choose the correct process related to it from the given list.
'Raw material is put in the blast furnace where it goes for smelting and then refined to obtain the output'.

options.html: The process of making cloth from fibres
The process of converting iron ore to steel

questions.explanation: It is the process of conversion from iron ore to steel in a blast furnace.

questions.question: With respect to the iron and steel industry,  Analyse and choose from the given list what this image depicts?

options.html: How the iron and steel industry paved the way for industrialization
The changing location of the iron and steel industry

questions.explanation:
It narrates the changes that took place in the location of iron and steel industry since 1800s.

  1. The best location before 1800 {Near river but small in size}
  2. The best location from 1800 to 1950 {Near Mountains}
  3. The best location since 1950 {Near river with systematic planning}

questions.question: India (2007-2008) is the ________ largest producer of steel in the world.
questions.explanation: Because India also has the Iron and steel industries since the early 1800s.

questions.question: Name the largest producer of steel in the world.
questions.explanation: China is the largest producer of steel in the world.

questions.question: Which place in the USA is called as the original centre of steel making and is slowly loosing ground and is now known as the dust bowl of America.
questions.explanation: Because today very few of the large steel mills are in Pittsburgh itself. They are located in the valleys of the Monogahela and Allegheny rivers above Pittsburgh and along the Ohio River below it.

questions.question: Name the important and the only iron and steel plant in India before 1947.

options.html: Tata Iron and Steel Company Limited (TISCO)

questions.explanation: Before 1947, there was only one iron and steel plant in the country - Tata Iron and Steel Company Limited (TISCO). It was privately owned. After Independence, the government took the initiative and set up several iron and steel plants. TISCO was started in 1907 at Sakchi, near the confluence of the rivers Subarnarekha and Kharkai in Jharkhand.

questions.question: Name the important place, shaded region on the India map, which is famous for its steel centre production?
questions.explanation: Salem is a city in Salem district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

questions.question:
Why was Jamshedpur chosen to set up the steel plant? Pick out the most appropriate answer from the given options.

  1. Abundant iron ore deposits
  2. Abundant coal and manganese deposits
  3. Availability of limestone and dolomite
  4. Cheap labour
  5. Direct link to major markets and ports of Kolkatta and Mumbai
questions.explanation: Because this place was only 32 km away from Kalimati station on the Bengal-Nagpur railway line. It was close to the iron ore, coal, and manganese deposits as well as to Kolkata which provided a large market. TISCO gets coal from Jharia coalfields, and iron ore, limestone, dolomite, and manganese from Odisha and Chhattisgarh. The Kharkai and Subarnarekha rivers ensured sufficient water supply. Government initiatives provided adequate capital for its later development.
